questions.questionText: Choose the wrong statement for zero error and zero correction.
options: If the zero of the vernier scale does not coincide with the zero of the main scale then the instrument is said to be having a zero error.
Zero correction has a magnitude equal to zero error but sign is opposite to that of the zero error.
Zero error is positive when the zero of vernier scale lies to the left of the zero of the main scale.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
There are two Vernier callipers both of which have 1 cm divided into 10 equal divisions on the main scale. The Vernier scale of one of the callipers ($C_1$) has 10 equal divisions that correspond to 9 main scale divisions.
The Vernier scale of the other calliper ($C_2$) has 10 equal divisions that correspond to 11 main scale divisions.
The readings of the two callipers are shown in the figure. The measured values (in cm) by callipers $C_1$ and $C_2$, respectively, are
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
In ordinary Vernier callipers, 10th division of the Vernier scale coincides with 9th division of the main scale. In a specially designed Vernier callipers the Vernier scale is so constructed that 10th division on it coincides with 11th division on the main scale. Each division on the main scale equals to 1 mm. The callipers have a zero error as shown in the figure-I. When the Vernier Calliper is used to measure a length, the concerned portion of its scale is shown in figure-II.

(A) Zero error in the callipers has magnitude 0.7 mm .

(B) The length being measured is $\mathbf{1 . 1 8 ~ c m}$.

(C) The length being measured is $\mathbf{1 . 2 2 ~ c m}$.

(D) Zero error in the callipers has magnitude 0.3 mm .
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The smallest division on the main scale of a vernier callipers is 1 mm, and 10 vernier divisions coincide with 9 main scale divisions. While measuring the diameter of a sphere, the zero mark of the vernier scale lies between 2.0 and 2.1 cm and the fifth division of the vernier scale coincides with a mark on main scale. Then diameter of the sphere is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The vernier constant of a vernier callipers is 0.1 mm and it has zero error of $-0.05$ cm. While measuring the diameter of a sphere, the main scale reading is $1.7$ cm and coinciding vernier division is $5$. The correct diameter will be $n \times 10^{-2}$ cm. Find $n$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The diagram shows part of the vernier scale of a pair of vernier callipers. Which reading is correct?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: What is the reading of vernier scale shown is figure?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In a vernier callipers, ten divisions of the vernier scale is equal to the length of nine divisions on the main scale. If the smallest division on the main scale is half millimeter, then the vernier constant is:
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A vernier callipers has 20 divisions on the vernier scale, which coincide with 19 divisions on the main scale. The least count of the instrument is 0.1 mm. The main scale divisions are of
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A vernier callipers having 1 main scale division = 0.1 cm is designed to have a least count of 0.02 cm. If n be the number of divisions on vernier scale and m be the length of vernier scale, then
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The main scale of a vernier callipers reads in millimeter and its vernier is divided into $\mathbf{1 0}$ divisions which coincides with 9 divisions of the main scale. The reading for shown situation is found to be $\frac{12 x}{10} \mathrm{~mm}$. Find the value of $\mathbf{x}$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A vernier callipers has a least count of 0.1 mm. The main scale reading just to the left of the zero mark of the vernier scale is 10, and the zero division of the vernier scale exactly matches a division on the main scale. If one main scale division equals 1 mm, find the measured length in cm.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In an experiment of simple pendulum, time period measured was 50 s for 25 oscillations when the length of the simple pendulum was taken as 100 cm . If the least count of stop watch is 0.1 s and that of meter scale is 0.01 cm , then the maximum possible percentage error in the calculated value of $\mathbf{g}$ is P. Then Find the value of 100P.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The density of a cube is calculate D by measuring its mass and the length of its sides. If the maximum errors in the measurement of mass and length are $3 \%$ and $2 \%$ respectively, then the maximum error in the value of density is $\_\_\_\_$ $\%$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The radius of a sphere is measured to be $5.3 \pm 0.1 \mathrm{~cm}$. Calculate percentage error in volume. Round off to nearest integer.
questions.subject: Physics
